name: Checkerboard Squares
recipeInstructions:
CHECKERBOARD SQUARES
1. Melt chocolate or chocolate icing and white icing.
2. In a large bowl of electric mixer, combine the cookie mix, egg, and vanilla. Mix at low speed until the mixture forms a dough.
3. Divide the dough in half. Remove one half from the bowl.
4. To the dough in the bowl, add cocoa powder; knead to mix well.
5. On a work surface, roll each half of the dough on separate sheets of waxed paper to form rectangles measuring 9x6 inches.
6. Using the waxed paper to lift the dough, invert the plain dough onto top of the chocolate dough. Remove the waxed paper from the plain dough.
7. Using a long chef's knife, cut the doughs lengthwise into strips measuring 2 inches wide.
8. Stack the strips, removing the waxed paper and alternating colors.
9. Cut the stack lengthwise into three equal strips.
10. Stack the strips so that colors alternate; gently press to ensure dough layers stick together.
11. Wrap the stack in plastic wrap; refrigerate for 2 hours.
12. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
13. Lightly grease two baking sheets.
14. Remove the plastic wrap from the dough; cut the stack crosswise into slices measuring 1/4 inch thick.
15. Place the slices checkerboard side down on the prepared baking sheets.
16. Bake for 8 minutes or until golden.
17. Transfer to a wire rack to cool.
18. With a pastry bag and writing tip, decorate cookies with dots of chocolate and icing.
Makes 4 dozen cookies.
